news.description: Maine Democratic Senate candidates Nirav Shah, Jordan Wood, Shenna Bellows and Troy Jackson discuss how they would oppose Sen. Susan Collins if they are chosen as the new candidate after Graham Platner suspended his campaign.

news.description: Four of the candidates competed over who would go furthest to overhaul a Supreme Court they blamed for the loss of abortion rights nationally.

news.title: In the wake of Graham Platner’s success (and fall), a candidate to replace him changes his mind about ‘genocide’
news.description: (JTA) — When Jordan Wood vied last fall for the Democratic nomination for Maine’s U.S. Senate seat, he avoided accusing Israel of genocide, citing a link between rising antisemitism and “the language” that people use. Graham Platner, who went on to overwhelmingly win the nomination, did not stint on using the term. Platner is out... news.title: Contender to replace Graham Platner as Senate candidate changes mind on ‘genocide’

Portland, 7/17/26

Went down to see Troy Jackson this evening under a beautiful, blue summer sky with Casco Bay as backdrop.

Got a free t-shirt and sign to promote him, listened to the speakers, shook hands after, spoke briefly, and left enthused.

#MEpolitics

In honor of the Odyssey, can we take a moment to acknowledge that Joshua Jackson has dated two Helen’s of Troy?

https://www.fxoxo.com/307506/

He dated Diane Kruger from 2005 to 2016 and Lupita Nyong’o 2023-2024. Diane was Helen in the movie Troy (2004) and Lupita was Helen in The Odyssey …

Democrat Troy Jackson would seem to be going into the Senate nominating convention with a decent lead among Maine Democratic delegates.

Encouraging for him, and possibly a nightmare scenario for Susan Collins as Jackson held a pretty Trumpy deep red district in the State Senate.
FWIW, Troy Jackson has a list of delegates to vote for if you’re firmly in his camp; I imagine other candidates will do or have done likewise:
if Platner had turned out not to be as big of a monster I was kinda hoping he'd be a bit Butler-esque in congress - though at this rate we may kind of get it from Troy Jackson anyway?

I am officially on duty today as delegate for special election . But there is no sanctity in this process. Being forced into this and forced to choose candidates you already ruled out is crazy. And yes Troy Jackson is another centrist , republican like Fetterman. Don’t trust him.
I watched an interview of Troy Jackson yesterday and I wasn't impressed, but I don't live in Maine.

Thanks for asking, I've been watching the process & suddenly nothings reported! Andrea LaFlamme had a small showing in the 1st primary so I hope they go that direction. But Troy Jackson keeps coming up in posts & he's just platner light so please sway them if u can!

Mainers, you’ve got about 1hr to register to vote in county caucuses to replace Platner.  Cumberland, I’d love your vote for delegate to the convention. I’m for Troy Jackson. I’ve seen him fight in real time for the platform that got 70% of the vote in the primary.

mainedems.org/senate-race/...

For those who are wanting @troyjackson207.bsky.social for Senate, his campaign has compiled a complete list of county nomination meetings with a list of delegates and alternates who are supporting him that he's asking us to vote for.

www.jacksonformaine.com/caucus

Given that most of the primaries have already happened, the only one I can think of is Troy Jackson in Maine.

He's not as good a speaker as Graham Platner, but he has every other advantage without being a piece of shit. His trying to distance himself from Platner also helps.

A new WaPo-Ipsos poll showed Trump’s job approval at 37%, with 61% disapproval, including 71% disapproval among independents. Polymarket odds favor Abdul El-Sayed in Michigan, Troy Jackson in Maine, and Peggy Flanagan in Minnesota Democratic Senate primaries.

As a Maine voter, I'd be perfectly happy with Troy Jackson as the nominee. He's been in the (Maine) public eye long enough that skeletons are unlikely to emerge from his closet.

But job #1 is to vote Susan Collins out and never have to hear her voice "concerns" and then vote for evil things.

Jackson's campaign has delegate slates of delegates and advises voting ONLY for them.

That's helpful to them but it also cues non-Troy supporters to not vote for them and excludes folks (like me) with multiple preferences and open minds. #mepolitics. www.jacksonformaine.com/caucus?fbcli...
With the large majority of the delegate pool still undeclared, the current tally measures which campaign has built the most public caucus operation. www.bangordailynews.com/2026/07/17/p...

He is.  Long history of public service in Maine, including as majority leader of the state senate.

And if I'm understanding the polls correctly, everyone gravitating to him as the candidate is very good news for getting rid of Collins.

en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Troy_Ja...
People still in their goddamn feelings about Maine should probably be asking why Sanders intervened in the first place and why on earth his word was enough for you. (He's not a Democrat!)

Interesting to now the Maine DSA fully enforced Troy Jackson for governor (meaning donor support) but only "recommended" Platner (with no donor support)-- kind of a "well, I guess he'll do, since he's what we've gotten stuck with."

news.description: Argentina and Spain are set to face off in the FIFA World Cup final on Sunday. NBC’s Monica Alba sits down with co-chairs of the Congressional Soccer Caucus, Rep. Kathy Castor (D-Fla.) and Rep. Darin LaHood (R-Ill.) to discuss the historic tournament in the latest installment of NBC's Common Ground series.

news.title: New analysis moves six House races towards Democrats ahead of midterms
news.description: The Cook Political Report shifts six House races in favor of Democrats. Editor Erin Covey joins Meet the Press NOW to explain the moves with four months to go until the midterms.

news.title: Maine Senate debate: Jackson, Bellows, Shah's chances of replacing Platner
news.description: Maine Democrats are scrambling to select a new Senate candidate to face off against Susan Collins after Graham Platner's exit.

news.title: WH says Trump speech will unveil new findings about 2020 elections that will ‘shock’ Americans
news.description: President Trump is set to deliver a primetime address to the nation, with a source familiar with the speech telling NBC News it will focus in part on the 2020 election. NBC’s Julie Tsirkin, Ryan Nobles and Jane Timm report on what to expect and the reaction by lawmakers ahead of the speech.

news.title: Maine ICE shooting puts Sen. Susan Collins in a political bind
news.description: PORTLAND, Maine >> Republican U.S. Sen. Susan Collins received a political gift when the withdrawal of her opponent Graham Platner threw the Maine Democratic Party into chaos. Then Immigration and Customs Enforcement fatally shot a man at a traffic stop in the coastal town of Biddeford.

news.title: Would-Be Platner Replacements in Maine Rally Around “Abolish ICE” (or Something Close)
news.description: After ICE killed Maine father Johan Sebastián Guerrero, most of the serious candidates to replace Platner called for abolishing or “dismantling” ICE. The post Would-Be Platner Replacements in Maine Rally Around “Abolish ICE” (or Something Close) appeared first on The Intercept .

news.title: More than a dozen Maine Democrats are running for Senate
news.description: The list includes many names that will be familiar to Maine Democrats — such as Troy Jackson, Nirav Shah and Shenna Bellows — but also some political newcomers. The party will select a nominee on July 25.
